name: Guan Yu Full
description: Guan Yu, also known as Guan Gong, is a revered historical figure in Chinese culture, elevated to the status of a deity in Taoism, Confucianism, and Chinese Buddhism. Originally a general serving under the warlord Liu Bei during the late Eastern Han dynasty, Guan Yu is celebrated for his unparalleled loyalty, righteousness, bravery, and strength. His most famous weapon is the Green Dragon Crescent Blade, a guandao of immense weight and power. Guan Yu played a crucial role in many key battles, including the Battle of Red Cliffs and the defense of Jing Province. Despite facing betrayal and eventual capture and execution by Sun Quan's forces, his unwavering commitment to his sworn oath with Liu Bei and Zhang Fei solidified his legendary status. After his death, Guan Yu's spirit was said to manifest and aid those in need, leading to his deification. He is now worshipped as a god of war, wealth, and protection, with temples dedicated to him found throughout China and beyond. Guan Yu embodies the virtues of loyalty, integrity, and courage, serving as a moral exemplar for generations. His story is prominently featured in the historical novel Romance of the Three Kingdoms, further cementing his place in popular culture and legend. He is often depicted with a red face, long beard, and imposing stature, wielding his iconic weapon and symbolizing righteousness and power. Guan Yu's influence extends beyond religious worship, impacting business practices, martial arts traditions, and even political ideologies. His legacy is a testament to the enduring power of virtue and the profound impact of historical figures on cultural identity.
subtitle: The God of Loyalty and Righteousness
achievements: Mastery of the Green Dragon Crescent Blade

keyMyths.title: Peach Garden Oath
keyMyths.description: Guan Yu, Liu Bei, and Zhang Fei swore an oath of brotherhood in a peach garden, vowing to support each other until death. This oath became a cornerstone of Guan Yu's legendary loyalty and his commitment to Liu Bei's cause.

keyMyths.title: Passing Five Passes and Killing Six Generals
keyMyths.description: After being separated from Liu Bei and captured by Cao Cao, Guan Yu eventually left Cao Cao to rejoin Liu Bei. He had to fight his way through five passes, killing six generals who tried to stop him, demonstrating his unmatched combat prowess and determination.

keyMyths.title: Deification as Guan Gong
keyMyths.description: After his death, Guan Yu's spirit was said to haunt those who wronged him, leading to temples being built in his honor. He became worshipped as a god of war, wealth, and protection, known as Guan Gong, solidifying his divine status.

sources.author: Luo Guanzhong
sources.description: A historical novel that greatly popularized Guan Yu's story and cemented his legendary status.
sources.text: A historical novel that greatly popularized Guan Yu's story and cemented his legendary status.
